records.ety: [OE. fauchon, OF. fauchon, LL. fälcio, fr. L. falx, falcis, a sickle, cf. Gr. <?/<?/<?/<?/<?/<?/ a ship's rib, <?/<?/<?/<?/<?/<?/ bandy-legged; perh, akin to E. falcon; cf. It. falcione. Cf. Defalcation.]

defs: A broad-bladed sword, slightly curved, shorter and lighter than the ordinary sword; -- used in the Middle Ages.
A name given generally and poetically to a sword, especially to the swords of Oriental and fabled warriors.
